steps.title: Use the left-end behavior
steps.content: The scatterplot is exponential and levels off near $y=18$ on the left. That points to a model with a vertical shift of $+18$, so the choices without that shift are less appropriate.

steps.title: Check the starting value
steps.content:
At $x=0$, the model should be near $24$. Compare the two shifted models:
$$24(1.55)^0+18=42$$
$$6(1.55)^0+18=24$$
The second shifted model matches the graph's starting level.

steps.title: Check the growth
steps.content:
At $x=3$, the same model gives
$$6(1.55)^3+18\sim40.3$$
That follows the plotted curve, while the shifted model with coefficient $24$ starts too high. The answer is D.
